body.u: When I was sixteen just about to leave school, I apliedapplied to a collage. It was for a T.V engineers course, and I got in.
I travelled there on a Monday morning by train, and every week day night I stayed at a friends. And then on Friday night I came home.

After a year I finished the course the proffesionprofession of a T.V. engineer. I was quite lucky in getting a job so quickly and so near home. At about that time 1980 there was a great {illegible} for them.

I got a job with “Brittion & Hobbs” (which by now had greatly expanded), in Sittingbourne High Street. It was an easy place to get to from the train. I was there for about three years, then I became the engineer in charge (which mentmeant more money).

After another five years there was a vacancy at the shop in Sheerness High Street, this was in 1988. The vacancy was for manager and I got the post. By this time I had moved (with my family) to a bigger house, but of course still in Minster.
While all this was going on I had been driving, I took my test and passed, so I got myself a car, an Austin Maxi.
All in all, I wasn’t doing to bad for myself.
I was still a bellringer at the local church and a faithfullfaithful member of the church, so faithfullfaithful in fact, that I contemplated seriously to give up all I had to join an order of monks.
After about ten years I was getting a bit fed up with circuit boards and components so I changed my job.
I joined coustomscustoms and exiceexcise at Dover. For this I done the same as before staidstayed at a friends during the week and on weekends I come home in the car.
This job was very minotinusmonotonous, so I changed my jobs once more.

I wanted to do something outside and not in doors, and I want a job were I didn’t have to wear overalls or a uniform. I want something I could do on my own and meet people. So I became a driving instructor.
Unfortunately this was not a regular job. I meant anytime in the day from about 7 until 10 which meant I had little time for myself. But I enjoyed it and stuck at it.
After a while I became a driving test examiner, which was a regular timed job. I remained that until I retired today.
